description: Simplify portal navigation - reduce tabs from 6 to fewer by consolidating views. Merge Usage into Dashboard, and merge Profile into a simpler layout. Customer portal should be simple and clean.
steps: 1. Consolidate the portal navigation in PortalShell.vue (panel/web/portal/src/layouts/PortalShell.vue). Current tabs: Dashboard, Billing, Usage, Support, VPN Profiles, Profile. Reduce to 4 main tabs: Dashboard (with usage info embedded), Billing, Support, VPN Profiles. Move Profile to a user dropdown/menu action, not a main tab. Usage data is already shown on the Dashboard (UsageGauge, progress bar, data stats) - the separate Usage page is redundant for most customers.

2. Update the router (panel/web/portal/src/router/index.ts): Keep the /usage and /profile routes (for direct URL access) but remove them from the main navigation tabs. Profile can be accessed via a dropdown on the username in the navbar. Add the profile link to the user actions area of PortalShell.

3. Enhance the portal DashboardView (panel/web/portal/src/views/DashboardView.vue) to include key session info from UsageView (active sessions list, bandwidth chart) so customers see everything they need on the dashboard without navigating to a separate usage page. Add a compact sessions section and a small bandwidth chart below the existing usage card.

4. In PortalShell.vue, replace the Profile link in the nav with a user dropdown that shows username and has a 'Profile Settings' option that routes to /profile. This keeps the nav cleaner while still providing access to profile.
acceptance_criteria: Portal navbar shows 4 main nav links: Dashboard, Billing, Support, VPN Profiles (not 6)
Profile is accessible via a user dropdown in the navbar actions area, not as a main tab
Usage route still works if navigated to directly (not removed from router, just from nav)
Portal Dashboard includes key usage/session data so a separate Usage tab is not needed
npm run build succeeds for portal
findings: Build passes cleanly (vue-tsc --noEmit && vite build). Used invisible backdrop element for click-outside dropdown close instead of a custom directive (no external library needed). Sessions section only shows when there are active online sessions. Router routes for /usage and /profile remain intact for direct URL access.
